name: BottomSheet
purpose: Displays supplementary content or actions anchored to the bottom of the screen.
whenToUse: Presenting a list of contextual actions (e.g., share menu)
Providing supplementary information without losing context of the underlying screen
whenNotToUse: Critical alerts requiring immediate user decision (use AlertDialog)
Complex, multi-step flows that require full screen focus

accessibility.semantics: Modal bottom sheets should trap focus and be dismissible via the Escape key or back button.
accessibility.dragHandle: If a drag handle is present, it should have a semantic label indicating it can be dragged to expand or dismiss.
accessibility.contrast: Ensure sufficient contrast between the bottom sheet surface and the underlying content, typically achieved via a scrim or shadow.

flutterMappingNotes: Use showModalBottomSheet() for the modal variant and Scaffold.showBottomSheet() for the standard variant.
Map container.backgroundColor to the backgroundColor property of the bottom sheet.
Map container.borderRadiusTop to the shape property using RoundedRectangleBorder with top-left and top-right radii.
Map scrim.color to the barrierColor property in showModalBottomSheet().
Use the showDragHandle property (available in Material 3) to automatically render a drag handle, mapping dragHandle tokens to BottomSheetThemeData.dragHandleColor and dragHandleSize.
